title: Quiz: VQE Optimization
description: Test your understanding of optimization techniques for VQE

questions.question: What is the main advantage of SPSA over gradient descent for VQE?

options.text: It always finds the global minimum
It requires only 2 function evaluations per iteration regardless of parameter count
It doesn't require a quantum computer

questions.explanation: SPSA (Simultaneous Perturbation Stochastic Approximation) approximates the gradient using only 2 function evaluations per iteration, regardless of the number of parameters. This is a major advantage for high-dimensional problems.

questions.question: What is the parameter-shift rule formula for computing ∂E/∂θ?

options.text: ∂E/∂θ = E(θ + ε) - E(θ) for small ε

questions.explanation: The parameter-shift rule gives exact gradients: ∂E/∂θ = [E(θ + π/2) - E(θ - π/2)] / 2. This works for gates of the form exp(-iθG/2) where G has eigenvalues ±1.

questions.question: How many circuit evaluations are needed to compute the full gradient for n parameters using the parameter-shift rule?
questions.explanation: The parameter-shift rule requires 2 evaluations per parameter (one at θ + π/2 and one at θ - π/2). For n parameters, this means 2n total circuit evaluations.

questions.question: Which optimizer is typically recommended for polishing VQE results near convergence?
questions.explanation: L-BFGS-B is a quasi-Newton method that uses gradient information to approximate the Hessian. It has fast convergence near the minimum, making it ideal for polishing after initial exploration phases.

questions.question: Shot noise in VQE scales as 1/N_shots, where N_shots is the number of measurements.
questions.explanation: False. Shot noise (standard error) scales as 1/√N_shots, not 1/N_shots. This is due to the central limit theorem - averaging N independent measurements reduces the standard deviation by √N.

questions.question: What is the purpose of adaptive shot allocation in VQE optimization?

options.text: To reduce the total number of measurements
To use few shots for exploration and more shots near convergence
To eliminate the need for optimization

questions.explanation: Adaptive shot allocation uses fewer shots during initial exploration (when precision is less critical) and more shots near convergence (when fine differences matter). This balances speed and accuracy.

questions.question: What is warm-starting in VQE?

options.text: Running the quantum computer at higher temperature
Initializing parameters from a related previous calculation
Using thermal states instead of pure states

questions.explanation: Warm-starting means initializing VQE parameters from a related calculation (e.g., a nearby geometry point, smaller basis set, or similar molecule). This provides a better starting point than random initialization.

questions.question: Which are valid convergence criteria for VQE? (Select all)

options.text: Energy change between iterations below threshold

questions.explanation: Valid convergence criteria include: (a) small energy change |Eₖ - Eₖ₋₁| < ε, (b) small gradient ||∇E|| < ε, and (d) small parameter change ||θₖ - θₖ₋₁|| < ε. Energy rarely equals zero exactly.
